title: How to Sell Ebooks from Kenya: A Step-by-Step Setup Guide — The Full Guide
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: Selling digital products from Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, or Bangladesh is possible with crypto-native infrastructure that has no geographic restrictions.

content.text: In Kenya, like many African countries, digital creators face a major obstacle when trying to sell ebooks: payment processors often block transactions due to geographic restrictions or banking limitations. If you're a Kenyan ebook author, you're probably familiar with the frustration of trying to use platforms like PayPal or Stripe, only to be met with an error message. You're not alone. Many creators in Kenya and around the world have been left in the dark, unable to monetize their digital products. That's where Unchained Commerce comes in, offering a solution that bypasses these restrictions and gets you paid.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

Our solution, the Full Stack Bundle, provides a complete store infrastructure that includes the Core Engine, Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard. By using blockchain-based payments through NOWPayments, you can instantly verify transactions and deliver your ebooks to customers without the need for a traditional payment processor. This setup works even in areas with strict banking regulations or limited access to payment services.
What You Need Before You Start

Before diving in, you'll need to sign up for the Full Stack Bundle. This will give you access to all the necessary tools and infrastructure to set up your store. Additionally, you'll need to familiarize yourself with the Admin Dashboard, where you can manage orders, track analytics, and monitor transactions. Don't worry if this seems overwhelming – we'll guide you through each step of the process.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

With the Full Stack Bundle, setting up your payment and delivery pipeline is a breeze. Simply connect your NOWPayments account to the Admin Dashboard, and you'll be able to process transactions and deliver your ebooks instantly. You can even set up a custom payment page and QR code to make it easy for customers to pay. By using the Wallet Connect feature, you can accept a wide range of cryptocurrencies, giving your customers more options for payment.
You can sell ebooks from Kenya without a traditional payment processor by using blockchain-based payments.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

Once you've set up your store and payment pipeline, it's time to go live. Upload your ebooks to the Core Engine, and make sure everything is configured correctly. When a customer places an order, the Admin Dashboard will automatically process the payment and deliver the ebook. You'll receive a notification and can track the order from your dashboard. It's that simple!
Scaling Once the System Is Running

As your store grows, you'll want to scale your operations to keep up with demand. With the Admin Dashboard, you can easily monitor analytics and adjust your setup as needed. You can also use the priority updates and Discord support included in the Full Stack Bundle to get help from our team whenever you need it. By following these steps, you'll be well on your way to building a successful ebook store that reaches customers all over the world.
How can creators in Africa sell digital products internationally?

The most practical path for creators in restricted countries is a combination of NOWPayments for payment processing and USDT on Tron for the primary payment coin. USDT is a stable coin pegged to the US dollar, which means your revenue does not fluctuate with crypto market movements. Tron network fees are under one dollar, and settlement takes under 60 seconds. Once you receive USDT in your wallet, you convert to local currency through P2P exchanges — Binance P2P, Paxful, or local platforms specific to your country. The conversion typically takes 20-30 minutes and costs 2-5% of the transaction, which is significantly less than PayPal's international transfer fees or Western Union's rates. This is the complete payment flow that thousands of creators in Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Pakistan, and Bangladesh are using right now.
